title: tournefortia coriacea
intro: Tournefortia coriacea is a plant species commonly known as the "Molave" or "Kapok", belonging to the family Boraginaceae. This plant has been found to have various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing skin conditions, and treating respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Tournefortia coriacea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, from colds and coughs to skin irritations and wounds. Additionally, the plant's resin has been used in traditional medicine to treat digestive problems and as an expectorant to relieve chest congestion.
